Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 15:02 ET
P/C split signals institutional dispersion hedge as negative gamma amplifies pre-CPI volatility
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Index puts heavy (1.62) while single-names see call demand (0.84) — classic institutional dispersion hedge into CPI Wednesday.
  • 2. Negative gamma at -$5.1B on SPY creates amplification risk with major data prints 3 days away.
  • 3. Energy sector seeing massive put spreads while financials attract call flow — macro divergence play ahead of rate-sensitive data.
NARRATIVE — Pre-CPI Dispersion Hedge

The most significant structural signal today is the extreme P/C divergence between indices and single names, with index P/C at 1.62 (heavy put demand) while single-name P/C sits at 0.84 (call-heavy flow). This divergence of 0.78x represents institutional dispersion hedging — buying index protection while expressing bullish single-name views ahead of Wednesday's CPI print. The positioning makes tactical sense given implied correlation at just 0.19, creating optimal conditions for stock-picking while hedging systematic risk. Dealer positioning amplifies the setup risk with SPY net gamma at -$5.1B, meaning any directional move gets mechanically amplified by dealer hedging flows. The gamma flip sits just $5 above spot at $663, creating a narrow band where small moves could trigger outsized volatility expansion. This negative gamma environment transforms Wednesday's CPI from a standard data event into a potential volatility explosion catalyst. Energy sector flow tells a macro story, with massive XLE put spreads (23K Sep 57.5P and 45P) suggesting positioning for oil price weakness, while financials see the opposite with 40K XLF Jun 53C blocks. This sector divergence aligns with rate cut expectations — higher rates benefit banks while pressuring energy demand. The positioning intensity ahead of CPI suggests institutions are making leveraged bets on the Fed's reaction function rather than the headline print itself.

Layer 1: Sentiment -18Layer 2: GEX -$5.1BLayer 3: P/C split 0.78x divergenceLayer 9: CPI in 3d
